question.text: Gold ETFs are allowed to invest in gold deposit schemes of banks up to what percentage of the net assets of the scheme?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Gold ETFs hold gold in the form of physical gold of 99.5% purity or gold receipts/exchange traded derivatives. They are allowed to invest up to 20% of net assets in gold deposit schemes of banks. Each ETF unit typically represents a proportion of weight of gold. **The Trap:** The 20% limit is specific to bank gold deposit schemes — the rest must be in physical gold or gold receipts. **Cross-Exam Context:** Gold ETF specifications are tested in V-A and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Gold ETFs are more convenient than physical gold but require a demat account — for clients without demat, Gold Fund of Funds (which invest in Gold ETFs) offer the same exposure without needing demat.
